items.question: Which identity defines the posterior up to normalization?
items.explanation: Bayes' theorem: posterior proportional to likelihood times prior; P(x) normalizes.

items.question: Beta(2,2) prior, 6 heads in 10 flips (Bernoulli). What is the posterior distribution?
items.explanation: Conjugate update: add successes to first shape (2+6=8) and failures to second (2+4=6).

items.question: As sample size n → ∞ with a fixed proper prior, what happens to the posterior?
options: It typically concentrates on values that maximize likelihood (under regularity)
It becomes uniform regardless of data
items.answer: It typically concentrates on values that maximize likelihood (under regularity)
items.explanation: Likelihood dominates with enough data; Bernstein-von Mises intuition for beginners.

items.question: Gaussian prior on regression weights most directly corresponds to which penalty?
items.explanation: Squared log-prior terms become an L2 penalty in MAP objective.
